title: triticum flexum
intro: Triticum flexum, also known as wild rye or rough wheat grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, fever, and inflammation due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Triticum flexum include reducing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, alleviating menstrual cramps, and supporting immune function. Triticum flexum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
